questions.audioText: Woman: I think we should eat less meat for environmental reasons. The meat industry produces a lot of greenhouse gases. Man: I agree in principle, but I'm not sure I could become a vegetarian completely. Maybe we can start with meat-free Mondays.
questions.question: What compromise does the man suggest?
options: Having one meat-free day a week

questions.audioText: Man: I went to a live concert last night for the first time in years. The sound quality was amazing, and the crowd's energy was incredible. Woman: Live music really is something special. Recordings just can't capture that feeling.
questions.question: What does the woman think about live music compared to recordings?
options: Live music creates a unique feeling

questions.audioText: Coach: You've been training well all season, and I know you're nervous about the championship match. But remember, you've beaten this team before. Trust your skills and play as a team, not as individuals.
questions.question: What is the coach's main message to the team?
options: Trust their abilities and work together

questions.audioText: Woman: My phone battery dies so quickly now. I've had it for three years. Man: You might need a new battery, or it could be time for an upgrade. Technology moves fast, and older phones just can't keep up with new apps.
questions.question: What does the man imply about older phones?
options: They are not compatible with new apps
They last longer than new ones

questions.audioText: News report: The local government has announced plans to build a new cycling path through the city center. Supporters say it will encourage more people to cycle instead of drive, reducing traffic and pollution.
questions.question: Why do supporters want the new cycling path?

questions.audioText: Woman: I'm thinking of studying abroad next semester. Maybe in Spain or Germany. Man: That sounds exciting, but have you looked into the costs? Tuition and living expenses can be quite high. Woman: I've been saving up, and there are scholarships available.
questions.question: What concern does the man raise about studying abroad?
options: The cost of tuition and living

questions.audioText: Woman: You know, I used to be afraid of public speaking. I would avoid it at all costs. But my job forced me to present at meetings, and over time I got used to it. Now I actually enjoy it.
questions.question: What helped the woman overcome her fear of public speaking?
options: Being required to do it at work

questions.audioText: Man: I just finished reading a book about the history of the internet. It's fascinating how it started as a military project and became something that connects almost everyone on the planet. Woman: And now we can't imagine life without it.
questions.question: How did the internet originally begin?

questions.audioText: Woman: You've been playing that video game for hours. Don't you think you should take a break? Man: Just one more level. I'm almost at the final boss. Woman: You said that an hour ago.
questions.question: What can be inferred from the woman's last statement?
options: She believes he will stop soon
She thinks he has been playing too long
She wants to play the game too
She doesn't think the game is fun

questions.audioText: Radio talk show: Today we're asking our listeners: What skill should every young person learn before leaving school? We've had calls about cooking, managing finances, and basic first aid. What do you think is most important?
questions.question: What is the topic of the radio talk show?
options: Essential life skills for young people
